THOUGHT
The user wants me to fix a crash in the LLVM SLP Vectorizer.
The crash is an assertion failure in `BoUpSLP::isGatherShuffledEntry`.
The assertion is: `(TE->Idx == 0 || (TE->hasState() && TE->getOpcode() == Instruction::ExtractElement) || isSplat(TE->Scalars) || getSameValuesTreeEntry(TE->getMainOp(), TE->Scalars)) && "Expected splat or extractelements only node."`.

This assertion checks the properties of a `TreeEntry` (`TE`) when trying to form a gather operation. It expects that for any lane other than the first one (`TE->Idx != 0`), the value is either an `extractelement` from a vector, a splat (all scalars are the same), or comes from a `getSameValuesTreeEntry` (which seems to handle cases where multiple lanes use the same scalar value).

The backtrace shows the crash happens while vectorizing `icmp` instructions. The reproducer IR has a block with many `icmp` instructions. The operands of these `icmp`s are PHI nodes. Some of these PHI nodes are redundant, for example `%11` and `%12` are identical.

Let's analyze the operands of the `icmp`s.
`%c1.1.1.i.i.i.i = icmp eq i8 %11, 0`
`%c1.1.i.i.i.i = icmp eq i8 %12, 0`
Here, `%11` and `%12` are the same PHI node: `phi i8 [ %l1.i.i.i.i, %entry ], [ %4, %end ]`.
And `%4` is `phi i8 [ 0, %pre ], [ %l1.i.i.i.i, %entry ]`.
The control flow from `entry` goes to `end`, so `%4` becomes `%l1.i.i.i.i`.
Then from `end` to `if.end151.1.i.i.1.i.i.i`, so `%11` and `%12` become `%4`, which is `%l1.i.i.i.i`.
So both `icmp`s compare `%l1.i.i.i.i` with 0.

The SLP vectorizer tries to group these `icmp` instructions. When it builds its internal tree structure (`BoUpSLP::TreeEntry`), it might encounter a situation where it tries to vectorize, for example, `(icmp %11, 0)` and `(icmp %12, 0)`. Since `%11` and `%12` are the same value, this is a valid vectorization candidate. The vectorizer will try to build a vector from the operands `(%11, %12)`.

The problem arises when the vectorizer analyzes the operands `(%11, %12)`. It sees that `%11` and `%12` are the same PHI node. It creates a `TreeEntry` for `%11` at index 0, and for `%12` at index 1. When processing the entry for `%12` (at index 1), it finds that the scalar value is the same as for index 0. The `isGatherShuffledEntry` function is called to determine if this can be represented as a gather/shuffle.

The assertion fails because the `TreeEntry` for `%12` (at `Idx=1`) does not meet any of the allowed conditions. It's not an `extractelement`, it's not a splat across all lanes of the vector being built, and `getSameValuesTreeEntry` might not be finding the corresponding entry for `%11` correctly in all situations.

The core of the issue seems to be that when multiple lanes in a vector refer to the exact same scalar value (which is not a constant), the vectorizer needs to handle this gracefully. The `getSameValuesTreeEntry` function is supposed to do this by finding the first `TreeEntry` with that same scalar value.

Looking at the code for `isGatherShuffledEntry`:
```cpp
// If we have multiple users of the same value, we can just shuffle it.
if (getSameValuesTreeEntry(TE->getMainOp(), TE->Scalars))
